cases.system_prompt: Rewrite the user text as a Claude-ready prompt. Structure: <task> (one primary deliverable, one sentence), <context> (background facts only, no instructions), <constraints> (concrete + testable: length, format, tone), <output_format> (exact shape — Markdown headers, JSON keys, etc.). Keep each section short. No meta-framing, no preamble. Base every constraint on what the user actually asked for — never invent requirements they did not state. Return only the prompt.
cases.user_prompt: build a python cli that scans a folder of json files and reports invalid rows with line numbers

runs.raw_output:
<task>Build a Python CLI tool that scans a folder of JSON files and reports invalid rows with line numbers.</task>  
<context>A user wants to validate JSON data in a folder, identifying any malformed entries and listing their line numbers for debugging.</context>  
<constraints>Output must be under 500 characters. Use plain text with line numbers. Maintain a neutral, technical tone.</constraints>  
<output_format>Plain text list of invalid lines, e.g., "Line 12: Invalid JSON"</output_format>
runs.visible_output:
<task>Build a Python CLI tool that scans a folder of JSON files and reports invalid rows with line numbers.</task>  
<context>A user wants to validate JSON data in a folder, identifying any malformed entries and listing their line numbers for debugging.</context>  
<constraints>Output must be under 500 characters. Use plain text with line numbers. Maintain a neutral, technical tone.</constraints>  
<output_format>Plain text list of invalid lines, e.g., "Line 12: Invalid JSON"</output_format>

runs.raw_output:
<task>Build a Python CLI tool that scans a folder of JSON files and reports invalid rows with line numbers</task>
<context>Files are JSON arrays where each item represents a row; invalid rows are those that do not parse as valid JSON</context>
<constraints>Output must be under 500 characters, use plain text with line numbers, maintain technical but clear language</constraints>
<output_format>Each invalid row is listed as "Line X: Error message"</output_format>

cases.system_prompt: Rewrite the user text as a Claude-ready prompt. Structure: <task> (one primary deliverable, one sentence), <context> (background facts only, no instructions), <constraints> (concrete + testable: length, format, tone), <output_format> (exact shape — Markdown headers, JSON keys, etc.). Keep each section short. No meta-framing, no preamble. Base every constraint on what the user actually asked for — never invent requirements they did not state. Return only the prompt.
cases.user_prompt: compare LM Studio Lemonade Ollama and FLM for a local AI desktop app and show the tradeoffs

runs.raw_output:
<task>Compare LM Studio Lemonade Ollama and FLM for a local AI desktop app and outline key tradeoffs</task>  
<context>LM Studio Lemonade Ollama is a toolchain for running large language models locally using Ollama, while FLM (Fast Language Model] is a lightweight alternative designed for faster inference on local hardware.</context>  
<constraints>Maximum 500 words. Use Markdown. Tone: neutral, technical. Include pros and cons of each.</constraints>  
<output_format>  
- Use Markdown headers  
- Include key points under "Pros", "Cons", and "Tradeoffs"  
- Limit to 3 bullet points per section  
- Avoid markdown formatting in the final output  
</output_format>
runs.visible_output:
<task>Compare LM Studio Lemonade Ollama and FLM for a local AI desktop app and outline key tradeoffs</task>  
<context>LM Studio Lemonade Ollama is a toolchain for running large language models locally using Ollama, while FLM (Fast Language Model] is a lightweight alternative designed for faster inference on local hardware.</context>  
<constraints>Maximum 500 words. Use Markdown. Tone: neutral, technical. Include pros and cons of each.</constraints>  
<output_format>  
- Use Markdown headers  
- Include key points under "Pros", "Cons", and "Tradeoffs"  
- Limit to 3 bullet points per section  
- Avoid markdown formatting in the final output  
</output_format>

<task>Compare LM Studio Lemonade Ollama and FLM for a local AI desktop app and outline key tradeoffs</task>  
<context>LM Studio Lemonade Ollama is a toolchain for running large language models locally using Ollama, while FLM (Fast Language Model] is a lightweight alternative designed for faster inference on local hardware.</context>  
<constraints>Maximum 500 words. Use Markdown. Tone: neutral, technical. Include pros and cons of each.</constraints>  
<output_format>Markdown with headings: **Comparison**, **Pros**, **Cons**, **Tradeoffs**</output_format>

cases.user_prompt: write a polite email to a vendor asking for missing API credentials and a timeline

runs.raw_output:
<task> Request missing API credentials and a timeline from a vendor</task>  
<context> You need to contact a third-party vendor to obtain missing API credentials and ask for a timeline for their availability.</context>  
<constraints> 12 sentences maximum. Use formal but polite language. Include a subject line. Use sender’s name and company in the salutation.</constraints>  
<output_format>  
# Subject: Request for Missing API Credentials and Timeline  
Dear [Vendor Name],  
[Body of email with request]  
Sincerely,  
[Your Name]  
[Your Company</output_format>
runs.visible_output:
<task> Request missing API credentials and a timeline from a vendor</task>  
<context> You need to contact a third-party vendor to obtain missing API credentials and ask for a timeline for their availability.</context>  
<constraints> 12 sentences maximum. Use formal but polite language. Include a subject line. Use sender’s name and company in the salutation.</constraints>  
<output_format>  
# Subject: Request for Missing API Credentials and Timeline  
Dear [Vendor Name],  
[Body of email with request]  
Sincerely,  
[Your Name]  
[Your Company</output_format>

runs.raw_output:
<task>Request missing API credentials and a timeline for resolution</task>  
<context>Vendor has not provided required API credentials, causing delays in system integration. Follow-up is needed to ensure timely access.</context>  
<constraints>125 characters or less. Use formal but polite tone. Include request for credentials and timeline.</constraints>  
<output_format>Subject line followed by body paragraph in Markdown</output_format>

cases.user_prompt: analyze a CSV export of time logs and explain missing expected hours by person and date

runs.raw_output:
<task>Identify missing expected hours in time log data by person and date</task>  
<context>A CSV file contains time log records with fields: person, date, actual_hours. Expected hours are defined as 8 per day. The goal is to find days where actual_hours is less than 8 for each person.</context>  
<constraints>Output must be in a table with columns: person, date, missing_hours (calculated as 8 - actual_hours]. Missing hours must be non-negative. Each row must be a single entry.</constraints>  
<output_format>Markdown table with headers: person, date, missing_hours</output_format>

cases.user_prompt: design a small dashboard for model benchmarks with provider filters and failure states

runs.raw_output:
<task>Design a dashboard to display model benchmark results with provider filters and failure states</task>
<context>Benchmarking models across different providers to evaluate performance, including metrics like accuracy, latency, and error rates. Filters allow users to isolate data by provider, while failure states highlight models that failed during testing.</context>
<constraints>Max 500 words. Use bullet points or tables. Include visual indicators for failure states (e.g., red icons). Ensure clarity for non-technical users.</constraints>
<output_format>Markdown document with headers for Dashboard Overview, Provider Filters, Failure States, and Key Metrics. Include a table showing sample data.</output_format>

cases.user_prompt: create a rollout plan for switching providers with fallback and performance gates

runs.raw_output:
<task>Rollout plan for provider switch with fallback and performance gates</task>  
<context>Organisation is transitioning from Provider A to Provider B across multiple regions. The switch must be reversible with fallback to Provider A if issues arise. Performance gates will determine when to fully transition based on real-time metrics.</context>  
<constraints>Maximum 500 words. Use bullet points and numbered lists. Tone: professional but concise. Include phases, criteria, and rollback steps.</constraints>  
<output_format>Markdown with headers #, ##, and lists. No markdown in the final output.</output_format>

cases.user_prompt: review this pull request for regressions and missing tests before release

runs.raw_output:
<task>Review the pull request for potential regressions and missing test cases</task>
<context>The pull request introduces a new feature for handling large file uploads, with changes to the upload service and storage logic.</context>
<constraints>Output must be no longer than 250 words. Use concrete, testable language. Maintain a formal but concise tone.</constraints>
<output_format>Markdown headers only. Include a brief summary of findings, list any regression risks, and identify missing test cases in bullet points.</output_format>
